failure_modes.text: Subjective priors can make a mathematically tidy update land on a badly distorted conclusion.
failure_modes.mitigation: Anchor to the outside view: consult external reference classes to set the prior before adjusting for case-specific details.

failure_modes.text: Substitution bias can smuggle in an easier but wrong model instead of the one the problem actually needs.
failure_modes.mitigation: Enforce dialectic standards: require every model choice to meet its antithesis before accepting it as the update framework.

failure_modes.text: False precision can make the math decorative when the prior or evidence quality has not earned the rigor.
failure_modes.source_quote: Danger When the Math Becomes Decorative
failure_modes.mitigation: Practice intellectual humility: externalize the reasoning structure in diagrams or concept maps and verify that each input has earned its precision level.

premortem_questions.text: What assumptions undergird this initial probability?
What would we have to believe for the opposite hypothesis to be true?
premortem_questions.source_quote: "What would we have to believe for the opposite hypothesis to be true?"

premortem_questions.text: What specific facts must be true for this plan to succeed, and how certain are we of each fact?
premortem_questions.source_quote: "What specific facts must be true for this plan to succeed, and how certain are we of each fact?"

heuristics.text: Quantify the prior before collecting more evidence so later updates have a visible starting point.
Update iteratively so each posterior becomes the next prior instead of treating each new datapoint as a reset.
Anchor to the outside view and aggregated data before trusting the inside view story.

curation_notes.summary: The source is naturally chunk-ready on prior discipline, disconfirming challenge, and anti-decorative-rigor safeguards.
donor_drops: Did not preserve donor mathematical detail beyond the raw source's stronger intervention surface around priors, outside view, and disciplined updates.
open_questions: The final heuristic compresses the mitigation row into an actionable chunk so it can operate alongside base-rates without duplicating that file.

allies: The raw allies section is rich on scientific-method-evidence-testing and reference-class or outside-view discipline for later relation preservation.
antagonists: The raw antagonists section is strong on confirmation-bias, fast-thinking, and substitution bias, but only the cleanest canonical ids belong in Wave 3.
structured_tensions: The source explicitly preserves tensions against confirmation-bias and representativeness-heuristic.
